items.title: A session terminal shows in both the TERMINAL tab and the session strip
items.precondition: DevEasy running; a session open on a project.
steps: 1. In the session pane, add a terminal (the '+' in the terminal strip) — or have the agent call terminal_create.
2. Look at the session pane's terminal strip.
3. Open the project's editor TERMINAL tab.
items.expected: The terminal appears in BOTH the session strip and the project TERMINAL tab.

items.title: Two session terminals are tabbed below the browser
items.precondition: A session with the browser pane open.
steps: 2. Observe the strip below the browser.
items.expected: Both terminals appear as tabs below the browser pane; switching tabs shows each.

items.title: Pane reveals when a browser OR a session terminal becomes active
steps: 1. With the pane hidden, have the agent create a terminal (terminal_create) — pane should slide in showing the terminal.
2. Separately, have the agent navigate the browser — pane shows the browser region.
items.expected: The pane auto-reveals on either browser or terminal activity; browser on top, terminals below, each region shown when active.

items.title: Agent terminal tools create/run/read/close session terminals
steps: 1. Ask the agent to open a terminal and run a command (e.g. `echo hi`).
2. Confirm the terminal appears in the session strip and shows the output.
3. Ask the agent to close it.
items.expected: terminal_create/run/read/close drive real session terminals visible in the pane; a terminal not owned by the session is refused (TERMINAL_NOT_IN_SESSION).
items.notes: Exercises the unified /mcp terminal tools live.

items.title: Browser tools still work after the MCP rename (regression)
steps: 1. Ask the agent to open a URL in the browser (browser_navigate).
items.expected: The renamed /mcp 'deveasy' server still serves browser_* tools; the agent opens the page.

items.title: Session delete closes its terminals; stop leaves them
items.precondition: A session with one or more session terminals running.
steps: 1. Stop the session (end the CLI) — confirm the terminals keep running.
2. Delete the session — confirm its terminals (and browser) are closed.
items.expected: Stop leaves terminals running; delete closes the session's terminals and browser.

items.title: Run-and-preview skill launches the app and opens it
items.precondition: A session on a full-stack project whose servers are NOT already running.
steps: 1. Ask the session to 'run this app locally and open it'.
2. Watch the terminals start backend + frontend in the session strip.
3. Wait for the browser to open.
items.expected: The skill starts both servers in visible terminals, waits until the frontend port answers, then opens the browser on the running app — no manual steps.
items.notes: The skill must be discovered in the session's capabilities first (injected via .claude/skills).

items.title: No regression in plain project terminals / TERMINAL tab
steps: 1. Open a terminal from a project folder / the TERMINAL tab (no session).
items.expected: Project-only terminals (sessionId null) behave exactly as before; they do not appear in any session strip.
